products.title: Solar 'Globe' light, 30 diameter
products.body_html:
The smaller size of our popular 'silar globe' garden lights. Warm white garden light with an attractive warm-white light. Easy to instal and totally 'ready-to-go'. No wiring or electrician required.

High quality, made in Germany. Long lasting, reliable, durable. Can be left outside all year round. Many years of no-maintenance garden lighting.

Because the solar collecter is on a long cable, the globes can be placed in the desired location and the solar panel can be somewhere less prominent. Performance is based on the solar spike being in a brigt or sunny location. 

  • Powerful warm white light
  • Goes on automatically at dusk using built-in light sensor
  • On / off switch if you prefer not have the light go on
  • IP44 rated / fully weatherproof
  • UV resistant polyethelene
  • 400cm long cable for solar spike
  • Replacement solar and bulb unit available: please enquire

Dimensions: 30cm diameter globe. Includes 4 metre long cable and solar spike. Generally available in our Monkstown and Airfield / Dundrum location.

products.title: Margaret Stokes (1915-1996), Connemara Garden
products.body_html:
Woodcut depicting a west of Ireland garden, filled with plants, chickens and movement. Behind them is a low, dry-stone wall, with rolling mountains and clouds in the background.

Margaret Stokes was a well-regarded Dublin artist whose life spanned the majority of the 20th century. Known for her works in oil, watercolours and her prints. She was a niece of Mainie Jellet, who twas a teacher and mento to her in her youth. She was an art teacher at Alexandra College and exhibited widely throughout her life.

40 x 35cm excluding frame. Woodcut, 7/25. Signed botton right. Titled bottom left.

Artworks can be viewed and purchased in our Clare Street shop. Overseas shipping is available on request.

products.title: William Rothenstein (1872-1945), W. B. Yeats
products.body_html:
Portrait of the poet and playwright W.B. Yeats by English artist William Rothenstein. Rothenstein was a war artist, landscape painter and portraitist, who is best remembered now for his portarits of intellectuals and artists. He was the principal of the Royal College of Art in London from 1920 to 1935. He was knighted in 1931 for his services to art. Many of his pieces are now in collections of the Tate Gallery and the National Portriat Gallery, London.

This lithograph was made in 1898 when Yeats was just 23. Yeats continued to use this image as the frontispiece for many of his later Collected Works. Rothenstein produced a number of these hand-signed lithographs, copies of which are now in the Hugh Lane Gallery, The Victoria & Albert Museum and the National Portrait Gallery, London.

44 x 27cm. Signed and dated in the plate, [18]98. Also signed in pencil bottom right. In original frame with 'W. ISLE' sticker on the reverse.

Artworks can be viewed and purchased in our Clare Street shop. Overseas shipping is available on request.

products.title: Dasylirion longissimum / Mexican grass tree
products.body_html:
A most unusual 'grass tree' that is in fact officially classed as a succulent. The tufted grass-like leaves are a blue-green colour. As the plant matures, a small roughly textured stem appears.

Daysylirion requires excellent drainage and plenty of light. They are suitable for well-drained pots and planters, for raised beds or for stony / sandy soil in well-drained locations. They are generally hardy in terms of temperature. Good drainage is the most important factor to consider, in combination with a bright / sunny location.

Young plants are approx 30-40cm wide x high in a 3 litre container.

products.title: Olive tree / Olea europea (semi mature for screening)
products.body_html:
Tall, semi-mature olive trees which are ideal for fast, evergreen garden screening. Olive trees are hardy in the Irish climate and, once established, are easilyh maintained and suitable for trimming, if necessary. These tall specimens could be planted anything from 100 to 200cm apart. They will require staking while they establish.

Olive trees are an popular choice for the suburban Dublin garden. Evergreen, and easily trimmed. Drought and wind tolerant and not prone to pests or diseases. Easily grown in any bright, well-drained location. Fertilise regularly with tomato food. in the foirst 2 - 3 years after planting. 

These tall specimens come in a 35 litre container. Total height 325-350cm. Size and density varies between plants and may not be identical to the images shown. 

Currently available in our Monkstown location.

products.title: Acacia dealbata/ Mimosa / Wattle (semi-mature specimens)
products.body_html:
Tall, semi-mature specimens of Acacia dealbata / Mimosa trees, ideal for screening or creating instant evergreen height.

Acacia dealbata are medium-sized to large evergreen trees,  admired for their yellow flowers in late winter and spring, as well as its fern-like leaves. The flowers are small fluffy and yellow. Best grown in a bright lcation, in well-drained soil.

Around Dublin, Mimosa trees can grow to at least 4 metres in height. As this tree can grow fast, we recommend trimming the top / new growth for the first couple of years in order to develop a sturdy main trunk.

An ideal tree for creating evergreen screening and privacy, or as a specimen.

Tall semi-mature plants 300-350cm in a 25 litre pot. Plants will require staking. We recommend lightly trimming the top before planting.

Currently available in our Monkstown shop.

products.title: Joel Owen (1892-1931), Landscape of Mountains & Lake
products.body_html:
Landscape painting, signed Joel Owen and dated 1929. Depicted is a rugged western landscape, probably Scotland, possibly Ireland. A touch of mist drifts from the mountain tops, while the pink-golden clouds suggest dusk.

Joel Owen is a mysterious artist whose try identity is unknown. In fact, the name 'Joel Owen' is probably a pseudonym. It is believed that whoever the true 'Joel Owen' was was the same person who used multiple other pseudonyms. It is often thought that they are the same person as 'F. E. Jamieson' and 'W. Richards', both of whom we have sold in our Clare Street location. In any case, regardless of the pseudonym used, this artist was highly popular in their day, with many of their works being reproduced.

Oil on canvas. 40 x 19cm excluding frame. Signed and dated bottom left. In original gilt frame.

Artworks can be viewed and purchased in our Clare Street shop. Overseas shipping is available on request.

products.title: Mini 'Crean Mate' tool cleaner (Niwaki)
products.body_html:
A mini-sized of Niwaki's popular Crean Mate. Use Crean Mate like an eraser for cleaning garden tools, most especially cutting tools such as secateurs and shears. Sap, rust and dirt can build up on blades, making your tools sticky and hard to use. Use with a drop of water or camellia oil to remove grime as if you were using a rubber. Crean Mate has a rough texture so be careful if using on your most prized blades as it can scratch.

Made in Japan. Approx dimensions: 1.9 x 1.9 x 4.2cm

Generally available in our Dublin shops.

products.title: Kenzan (flower frog) Large (80mm diameter)
products.body_html:
Large kenzan ('flower frog') used for flower arranging or Ikebana flower displays. A good alternative to oasis as it is waste-free and endlessly reusable. Simply push a twig or flower stem into or between the brass spikes. Immerse in water in a vase or dish. 

  • 510g
  • 81mm
  • Brass/ Lead
  • Made in Japan
  • Includes Rubber Base

Generally available in our Monkstown & Clare Street shops.
